question: Based on the unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Minnesota and Montana likely differ in unemployment rate by more than Iowa and Nebraska do, even though Minnesota and Montana share the same color.
options.B: Minnesota and Montana have very similar unemployment rates because they are the same color.
options.C: All four states (Minnesota, Montana, Iowa, Nebraska) clearly have identical unemployment rates.
options.D: The unemployment rate differences between these states are impossible to compare from this map.

explanation: Minnesota (9.8%) and Montana (14.9%) have a 5.1% unemployment rate difference, while Iowa (2.7%) and Nebraska (3.3%) have only a 0.6% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Minnesota and Montana into the same color bin (9%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Iowa and Nebraska appear more different due to different color bins.
